originalCode:
# Skills & Agents

4 sources: Superpowers (primary), ECC (supplementary), UI/UX Pro Max (design), Karpathy Guidelines (quality).

Superpowers > ECC when both match. See `rules/superpowers-priority.md`.

---

## How Skills Work in Augment

- Skills are loaded from `~/.augment/skills/` into the agent's context at session start
- They are **not auto-executed** — the agent reads them and follows their guidance
- Activation happens 3 ways: you ask for it, the agent matches it to your request, or a rule triggers it
- Session lifecycle is automated by `~/.augment/rules/session-lifecycle.md` (Rules 0-4)

---

## Superpowers (14 skills, 1 agent) — PRIMARY

Core workflow skills that govern *how* the agent works. These fire for every project regardless of language.

### Workflow

| Skill | When | What it does |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `brainstorming` | Before any creative/feature work | Explores intent, requirements, and design before implementation |
| `writing-plans` | Have a spec or requirements | Produces step-by-step implementation plan before touching code |
| `executing-plans` | Have a written plan | Executes the plan in a session with review checkpoints |
| `subagent-driven-development` | Independent tasks in current session | Dispatches parallel sub-agents for each task |
| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | 2+ independent tasks | Runs them simultaneously instead of sequentially |

### Quality

| Skill | When | What it does |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `test-driven-development` | Implementing any feature or bugfix | Write tests first, then implement |
| `systematic-debugging` | Any bug, test failure, unexpected behavior | Structured hypothesis → targeted fix |
| `verification-before-completion` | About to claim work is done | Must run verification and show evidence before asserting success |
| `requesting-code-review` | Completing a feature, before merging | Reviews work against plan and standards |
| `receiving-code-review` | Got review feedback | Verify feedback technically before implementing — no blind agreement |

### Git & Completion

| Skill | When | What it does |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `finishing-a-development-branch` | Implementation complete, tests pass | Structured options: merge, PR, or cleanup |
| `using-git-worktrees` | Starting feature work needing isolation | Creates isolated git worktree with safety checks |

### Meta

| Skill | When | What it does |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `writing-skills` | Creating or editing skills | Skill authoring guidelines |
| `using-superpowers` | Starting any conversation | How to find and use skills |

### Agent

| Agent | What it does |
|-------|-------------|
| `code-reviewer` | Primary code review agent — overrides ECC's |

---

## ECC Skills by Category

### Agent & Automation (20 skills)

| Skill | What it does |
|-------|-------------|
| `ck` | Per-project persistent memory. Auto-handled by Rules 1 & 2 — no commands to remember |
| `continuous-learning` | Extract reusable patterns from sessions. Auto-handled by Rule 2 on session end |
| `continuous-learning-v2` | Instinct-based learning with confidence scoring and project scoping |
| `strategic-compact` | Context compaction at logical intervals. Auto-handled by Rule 3 |
| `autonomous-agent-harness` | Persistent memory, scheduled ops, task queuing for autonomous agents |
| `autonomous-loops` | Sequential pipelines to multi-agent DAG architectures |
| `continuous-agent-loop` | Quality gates, evals, and recovery controls for agent loops |
| `gan-style-harness` | Generator-Evaluator pattern for autonomous building |
| `agent-harness-construction` | Optimize action spaces, tool definitions, observation formatting |
| `agent-eval` | Head-to-head comparison of coding agents on custom tasks |
| `agent-payment-x402` | Per-task payment budgets and spending controls for agents |
| `agentic-engineering` | Eval-first execution, decomposition, cost-aware model routing |
| `ai-first-engineering` | Operating model for AI-heavy engineering teams |
| `claude-devfleet` | Dispatch parallel agents in isolated worktrees |
| `dmux-workflows` | Multi-agent orchestration via tmux pane manager |
| `team-builder` | Interactive agent picker for composing parallel teams |
| `ralphinho-rfc-pipeline` | RFC-driven multi-agent DAG with quality gates |
| `santa-method` | Two independent review agents must both pass before shipping |
| `iterative-retrieval` | Progressive context refinement for subagents |
| `context-budget` | Audit context window consumption, identify bloat |

### Architecture & Patterns (9 skills)

|-------|-------------|
| `api-design` | REST resource naming, status codes, pagination, versioning, rate limiting |

## UI/UX Pro Max (1 skill)

Searchable design database: **67 styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, 25 chart types** across 16 tech stacks.

The agent auto-queries this when you ask about design, styling, or UI decisions. You can also search directly:

```bash
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "fintech" --domain style -n 5
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "dashboard" --domain color -n 3
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "mobile" --domain ux -n 5
```

---

## Karpathy Guidelines (1 skill)

Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes, derived from [Andrej Karpathy's observations](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) on LLM coding pitfalls.

**The 4 core principles:**

| Principle | What It Fixes |
|-----------|---------------|
| **1. Think Before Coding** | Wrong assumptions, hidden confusion, missing tradeoffs |
| **2. Simplicity First** | Overcomplication, bloated abstractions (1000 lines when 100 would do) |
| **3. Surgical Changes** | Orthogonal edits, touching code you don't understand |
| **4. Goal-Driven Execution** | Leverage through tests-first, verifiable success criteria |

**When it's working:**
- Fewer unnecessary changes in diffs — only requested changes appear
- Fewer rewrites due to overcomplication — code is simple the first time
- Clarifying questions come before implementation — not after mistakes
- Clean, minimal PRs — no drive-by refactoring or "improvements"

**Source:** [multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills](https://github.com/multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills)

---

## ECC Agents (37)

Sub-agents the main agent can dispatch for specialized tasks.

### Language Reviewers (9 agents)

| Agent | Specializes in |
|-------|---------------|
| `typescript-reviewer` | Type safety, async correctness, Node/web security |
| `python-reviewer` | PEP 8, Pythonic idioms, type hints, security |
| `go-reviewer` | Idiomatic Go, concurrency patterns, error handling |
| `rust-reviewer` | Ownership, lifetimes, error handling, unsafe usage |
| `java-reviewer` | Layered architecture, JPA patterns, security, concurrency |
| `kotlin-reviewer` | Coroutine safety, Compose, clean architecture |
| `cpp-reviewer` | Memory safety, modern C++ idioms, concurrency |
| `csharp-reviewer` | .NET conventions, async patterns, nullable types |
| `flutter-reviewer` | Widget best practices, state management, Dart idioms |

### Build Resolvers (8 agents)

| Agent | Fixes |
|-------|-------|
| `build-error-resolver` | TypeScript/JavaScript build and type errors |
| `cpp-build-resolver` | CMake, compilation, linker issues |
| `dart-build-resolver` | Flutter compilation, pub dependency conflicts |
| `go-build-resolver` | Go build, vet, linter warnings |
| `java-build-resolver` | Maven/Gradle, Java compiler errors |
| `kotlin-build-resolver` | Gradle, Kotlin compiler errors |
| `rust-build-resolver` | Cargo build, borrow checker issues |
| `pytorch-build-resolver` | Tensor shape mismatches, CUDA, gradient issues |

### Domain Specialists (8 agents)

| Agent | Specializes in |
|-------|---------------|
| `architect` | System design, scalability, technical decisions |
| `planner` | Detailed implementation plans |
| `database-reviewer` | PostgreSQL query optimization, schema design |
| `security-reviewer` | OWASP Top 10, secrets, SSRF, injection |
| `performance-optimizer` | Bottlenecks, bundle size, memory leaks |
| `healthcare-reviewer` | Clinical safety, CDSS accuracy, PHI compliance |
| `doc-updater` | Documentation and codemap updates |
| `docs-lookup` | Up-to-date library docs via Context7 MCP |

### Testing & Quality (2 agents)

| Agent | Specializes in |
|-------|---------------|
| `tdd-guide` | Test-driven development methodology, 80%+ coverage |
| `e2e-runner` | E2E testing with Playwright, test journeys, flaky tests |

### GAN Harness (3 agents)

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `gan-planner` | Expands prompt into full product spec with sprints |
| `gan-generator` | Implements features, reads evaluator feedback, iterates |
| `gan-evaluator` | Tests live app via Playwright, scores against rubric |

### Open Source Pipeline (3 agents)

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `opensource-forker` | Fork project, strip secrets, replace internal refs |
| `opensource-sanitizer` | Scan for leaked secrets, PII, internal references |
| `opensource-packager` | Generate README, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, setup.sh |

### Operations (4 agents)

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `chief-of-staff` | Triage email, Slack, LINE, Messenger communications |
| `harness-optimizer` | Optimize agent harness for reliability, cost, throughput |
| `loop-operator` | Monitor autonomous loops, intervene when stalled |
| `refactor-cleaner` | Dead code cleanup, unused imports, consolidation |

---

## Data Storage

| What | Where | Why centralized |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Skills | `~/.augment/skills/{superpowers,ecc,ui-ux-pro-max,learned}/` | Auto-discovered by Augment |
| Agents | `~/.augment/agents/{superpowers,ecc}/` | Auto-discovered by Augment |
| Rules | `~/.augment/rules/` | Always loaded into every prompt |
| ck project memory | `~/.augment/ck/` | No repo pollution, survives project deletion |
| Learned patterns | `~/.augment/skills/learned/` | Available across all projects |
| Plugin sources | `~/.augment/plugins/marketplaces/` | Git repos for updates |

---

## Session Lifecycle (automated by rules)

```
Session start → Rule 1 fires → auto-resume or offer to register project
     ↓
  Do work → agent matches skills automatically
     ↓
  Git commit → Rule 2 fires → auto-save checkpoint
     ↓
  Say "bye" → Rule 2 fires → auto-save + continuous-learning evaluation
     ↓
  Next session → Rule 1 fires → full briefing with where you left off
```

No commands to memorize. See `~/.augment/rules/session-lifecycle.md` for full rule definitions.

## UI/UX Pro Max (1 skill)

Searchable design database: **67 styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, 25 chart types** across 16 tech stacks.

The agent auto-queries this when you ask about design, styling, or UI decisions. You can also search directly:

```bash
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "fintech" --domain style -n 5
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "dashboard" --domain color -n 3
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "mobile" --domain ux -n 5
```

## Karpathy Guidelines (1 skill)

Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes, derived from [Andrej Karpathy's observations](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) on LLM coding pitfalls.

**The 4 core principles:**

| Principle | What It Fixes |
|-----------|---------------|
| **1. Think Before Coding** | Wrong assumptions, hidden confusion, missing tradeoffs |
| **2. Simplicity First** | Overcomplication, bloated abstractions (1000 lines when 100 would do) |
| **3. Surgical Changes** | Orthogonal edits, touching code you don't understand |
| **4. Goal-Driven Execution** | Leverage through tests-first, verifiable success criteria |

**When it's working:**
- Fewer unnecessary changes in diffs — only requested changes appear
- Fewer rewrites due to overcomplication — code is simple the first time
- Clarifying questions come before implementation — not after mistakes
- Clean, minimal PRs — no drive-by refactoring or "improvements"

**Source:** [multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills](https://github.com/multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills)

## Data Storage

| What | Where | Why centralized |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Skills | `~/.augment/skills/{superpowers,ecc,ui-ux-pro-max,learned}/` | Auto-discovered by Augment |
| Agents | `~/.augment/agents/{superpowers,ecc}/` | Auto-discovered by Augment |
| Rules | `~/.augment/rules/` | Always loaded into every prompt |
| ck project memory | `~/.augment/ck/` | No repo pollution, survives project deletion |
| Learned patterns | `~/.augment/skills/learned/` | Available across all projects |
| Plugin sources | `~/.augment/plugins/marketplaces/` | Git repos for updates |

---

## Session Lifecycle (automated by rules)

```
Session start → Rule 1 fires → auto-resume or offer to register project
     ↓
  Do work → agent matches skills automatically
     ↓
  Git commit → Rule 2 fires → auto-save checkpoint
     ↓
  Say "bye" → Rule 2 fires → auto-save + continuous-learning evaluation
     ↓
  Next session → Rule 1 fires → full briefing with where you left off
```

No commands to memorize. See `~/.augment/rules/session-lifecycle.md` for full rule definitions.
